commands.review_summary:
Adds an iOS Health Export settings flow, HealthKit reader/background task, webhook upload pipeline, entitlements/plist/project wiring, and Swift tests.

PR surface: Other +1974. Total +1974 across 22 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defects by source inspection: the background export path reads anchor/pending files that the PR writes with complete file protection, and the app schedules Health Export background work before configuration. I did not run a locked-device HealthKit test.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because this adds a sensitive native capability and the remaining blockers include product/security approval and contributor-provided real behavior proof, not just a mechanical patch.

target.title: feat(ios): native HealthKit read + export to self-hosted webhook
target.body:
## Summary

Adds an **opt-in, privacy-first Health Export** module to the iOS app: native read-only HealthKit access that forwards selected metrics to a webhook the user runs on their **own private network**. No third-party services, no cloud middleman.

This is offered as an **optional contribution**. I understand the core declined a built-in HealthKit integration in #27181 — this keeps it fully opt-in, self-hosted, and isolated behind a new Settings screen, so it doesn't change default behavior for anyone who doesn't enable it. If the team would rather not carry this, that's totally fine; happy to keep it as a fork.

## What it does

- **Read-only HealthKit** (`toShare: []`): steps, heart rate, HRV (SDNN), active + basal energy, walking/running distance, sleep, and workouts.
- **Sleep** (`HKCategorySample`) is aggregated into a `SleepMinutes` metric.
- **Workouts** use `statistics(for:)` only — **no metadata, no GPS routes** are read.
- **Units are forced explicitly** per quantity type. HealthKit localizes units by region, which would otherwise make the exported payload schema unstable; forcing units keeps it deterministic.
- **Anchored reads** (`HKAnchoredObjectQuery`). The anchor advances **only after a successful 2xx POST**, so nothing is silently dropped on a failed upload.
- **Background delivery** via `HKObserverQuery` + `enableBackgroundDelivery`, plus a `BGProcessingTask` and a reconcile pass when the app returns to foreground.
- **Anti-exfiltration URL policy:** the webhook URL is validated to `https` + a `.ts.net` (Tailscale MagicDNS) host before it can be saved, so health data can only ever leave the device to a host on the user's own tailnet — never an arbitrary public endpoint. This is an opinionated default for the self-hosted use case and is documented in `HealthExportConfigStore` for maintainers who may want to relax/extend it.
- **Secrets handling:** the webhook token + URL live in the Keychain (`afterFirstUnlockThisDeviceOnly`); the larger (>4KB) HealthKit anchor lives in an encrypted file, not the Keychain.
- **Error handling:** 4xx are surfaced to the user without a retry loop (the user must fix token/URL); 5xx use exponential backoff.

## Wiring (minimal, additive)

- `project.yml` / `Info.plist`: link `HealthKit.framework`, add `NSHealthShareUsageDescription` **and** `NSHealthUpdateUsageDescription` (Apple requires both when HealthKit is linked, even read-only — App Store Connect rejects with `90683` otherwise), the `processing` background mode, and the `ai.openclaw.ios.healthexport` BGTask identifier (namespaced to match the existing `ai.openclaw.ios.bgrefresh`).
- `OpenClaw.entitlements`: `com.apple.developer.healthkit`.
- `OpenClawApp`: register/observe/schedule the BGTask and reconcile on `.active`.
- Settings: a new "Health Export" row + screen (`HealthExportSettingsView`).

All product changes are additive — the diff is **+1974 / -0**.

## Tests

`Tests/HealthExportTests.swift` covers URL validation (https + `.ts.net` allow/deny edge cases), payload shaping, sleep aggregation, the anchor-advances-only-on-2xx invariant, and the backoff state machine. Picked up automatically by the `OpenClawTests` target's `Tests/` glob.

`swiftformat --lint` and `swiftlint` (project configs) are clean for the new files.

## Companion server (OSS)

The webhook that receives the export is a small FastAPI service, MIT-licensed and open source: **`github.com/hernaninverso/openclaw-health`** (Bearer auth on every data endpoint, idempotent ingest, no GPS, loopback/tailnet only). The iOS client and the server are independent — the client just POSTs to whatever `.ts.net` webhook the user configures.

Closes the gap requested in #27181 for users who want it, without making it a core feature.
target.author: hernaninverso
